description: baseline Q4_K_M (3.2GB)
Q3_K_M + no-confidence prompt (current best)
Q3_K_S (quality dropped to 0.38)
IQ4_XS (slower gen than Q4_K_M)
CUDA binary with 0 GPU layers (3.6x slower)
flash-attn + kv-cache quant (HURT on unified memory)
ctx1024 + Q3_K_M + LoRA baseline (actual deployment config)
threads=1 broke output assembly (truncated JSON)
reasoning-budget=0: all valid JSON, 32% faster wall-clock, 1 case low score
ctx2048 + reasoning-budget=0: fixed complex case (0.98 score) but 6.5% slower gen
batch256/ubatch128 + noreasoning: hurt gen AND prompt speed
compact prompt + max_tokens=512: prompt too terse, killed quality
nostream + noreasoning + ctx1024: 4/5 cases perfect, complex case timeout (ctx limit)
ctx1536 + nostream + noreasoning: ALL 5 CASES PERFECT, 3.5% faster than baseline
fine-tuned Q3_K_M (3.0GB no LoRA): 6.4% slower than base+LoRA (2.5GB)
KV cache q4_0: 41% SLOWER, dequant overhead kills perf on unified memory
validation 3x: gen=1.19 (thermal throttle?), score=0.78 (intermittent quality)
post-cooldown retest: sustained gen ~1.14 (thermal), score 0.80 (complex case markdown fenced)
anti-markdown prompt: made things worse, only 3/5 cases, truncated outputs
parallel=1: fixed 4-slot KV bloat, 3/5 cases, score 0.99
ctx768: too small, all cases failed (prompts 287-435 tokens)
pre-LTO baseline parallel=1: only 1/5 case (build competing), but 1.494 gen tok/s BEST EVER
LTO + ARMv8.6a build: no gen improvement (1.179 vs 1.166), bandwidth-limited not compute-limited
speculative decoding: SKIPPED — Gemma family doesn't speculate well (models not distilled)
--json-schema {}: causes sampler init error (400 on all requests)
poll=0: gen same (1.180), but 5/5 valid JSON, score 0.98
MLC-LLM TVM q4f16_1: 5-8 tok/s decode (6x speedup). Correct chat output. No LoRA/structured output yet.
MLC-LLM TIR-only (no FlashInfer): 1.1 tok/s, no speedup over llama.cpp

c17 regex pre-parser (no LLM): 29/29 codes on bench (apps/mobile/convert/regex_preparser.py). Validates Rank 2; killer assumption (real CDA eICRs hide codes in XML attrs) untested — needs Rank 5 adversarial bench.
c17 regex pre-parser on adversarial-5 (no CDA XML support): 1/13 — confirms killer assumption from Rank 2.

c17 regex pre-parser + CDA XML attrs (OID 2.16.840.1.113883.6.96/.1/.88): 7/13 adversarial, 29/29 original 9, 36/42 combined = 0.857. 3 residual cases need LLM (zika displayname-only, chickenpox drug-name, strep narrative).

c17 Mac CPU llama-server v2 GGUF Q3_K_M, baseline 14-case bench: 35/42 = 0.833, 11/14 perfect. ALL 9 originals 29/29 = 1.00. 3 residual adversarial fails (zika displayname, chickenpox rxnorm, strep narrative) — knowledge gap, not training quality.

c17 Mac CPU llama-server v2 GGUF + GBNF grammar (apps/mobile/convert/cliniq_extraction.gbnf): 35/42 = 0.833 same score, 2-3x faster (40 vs 80 tokens, 0.8s vs 1.4s), eliminates "```json" fences and "SNOMED:" prefixes. On residual cases grammar HURTS — forces hallucination instead of honest natural-language fallback.

c17 regex pre-parser + CDA XML + curated lookup table (apps/mobile/convert/lookup_table.json, 42 aliases): 42/42 = 1.00 on combined 14-case bench, 14/14 perfect, no LLM. Three residuals resolved: Zika displayname (SNOMED 3928002, LOINC 78929-3), chickenpox RxNorm (197612 acyclovir 800 MG), strep narrative (SNOMED 43878008, LOINC 78012-2, RxNorm 723 amoxicillin).
c17 hybrid pipeline (apps/mobile/convert/hybrid_pipeline.py) — Mode A: regex+CDA+lookup, LLM if-empty. 42/42 = 1.00, 0 LLM calls. PRODUCTION WINNER.

c17 hybrid Mode B: regex+CDA only (lookup OFF), LLM grammar fallback. 36/42 = 0.857. LLM correctly invoked on zika+strep (deterministic empty) but fails to recover them — model lacks Zika/Strep displayName→code knowledge. Confirms lookup table is the actual fix; LLM can't substitute for missing domain coverage.

c17 hybrid Mode C: regex+CDA+lookup + always-merge LLM. 42/42 = 1.00 (LLM never adds wrong matches but in production would emit hallucinated codes outside the validator's expected set; not safe to ship).

c17 Swift port (apps/mobile/ios-app/ClinIQ/ClinIQ/Extraction/EicrPreparser.swift + LookupTable.swift): 42/42 = 1.00 on 14-case bench, identical to Python prototype. Build succeeds against iPhone17ProDemo simulator. ExtractionService.run() short-circuits the LLM when det.hasAnyDeterministic is true. Tier-3 lookup table inlined as Swift static let arrays (no Xcode resource plumbing).

c17 adv2 8-case stress bench (scripts/test_cases_adversarial2.jsonl) — deterministic-only: 3/21 = 0.143, 2/8 perfect. Failure modes confirmed: H5N1/mpox/RSV/Salmonella missing from lookup; TB acronym not aliased; ICD-10 B20 not parsed; drug names without dose unmatched; FALSE POSITIVE: "Tuberculosis ruled out" emitted SNOMED 56717001 (negation not detected — validator hides it because false-positives aren't penalized).

c17 adv2 8-case stress bench — LLM-only (Mac CPU GGUF v2 Q3_K_M, no grammar): 2/21 = 0.095. LLM hallucinates fake codes (H5N1 → 840810000, mpox → 423600001, etc.). Model lacks H5N1/mpox/RSV/Salmonella/TB-acronym/ICD-10→SNOMED knowledge. Grammar variant: 2/21 same (forces digits only, doesn't conjure correct ones). KEY FINDING: int4 vs int8 LLM upgrade (Rank 3) cannot fix this — bottleneck is knowledge coverage, not precision.

c17 NegEx-style negation detection (apps/mobile/convert/regex_preparser.py + apps/mobile/ios-app/ClinIQ/.../EicrPreparser.swift). 60-char before / 30-char after window, 13 triggers (ruled out, negative for, no evidence of, denies, without, etc.), terminated by .,;,but,however,newline,<. Case 7 (Tuberculosis ruled out) now emits only [31541009]. No regression on prior benches: originals + adv1 still F1=1.000 with 0 false positives. Validator now reports recall/precision/F1 separately. Ablation --no-negation surfaces the FP automatically.

c17 Fork E lookup table v0.2.0 — 4 new SNOMED reportables (H5N1, mpox, RSV, Salmonella), TB acronym + active pulmonary TB, HIV displayName variants. 5 new LOINC entries (H5 RNA, monkeypox DNA, RSV antigen, salmonella culture, HIV-1/2 ag/ab). 3 new RxNorm (oseltamivir, tecovirimat, ciprofloxacin) + bare-name aliases for 9 existing drugs. Bare ceftriaxone OMITTED to prevent dose-mismatch FP. Mirrored to Swift LookupTable.swift; xcodebuild PASS.

c17 FINAL deterministic-only sweep across 22 cases (originals + adv1 + adv2): Python 63/63 = F1=1.000, 0 FP, 22/22 perfect. Swift 63/63 = 1.000 identical. Adv2 went from 0.143 → 1.000 by extending lookup table; original benches held at 1.000. Total deterministic-path coverage: 14 SNOMED, 21 LOINC, 15 RxNorm aliases.

c17 Gemma 4 agentic function-calling (apps/mobile/convert/agent_pipeline.py) — base Gemma 4 E2B Q3_K_M on Mac CPU as orchestrator, 3 tools: extract_codes_from_text (regex+CDA+lookup+NegEx), validate_fhir_extraction, lookup_displayname. 22/22 cases perfect, F1=1.000, 0 FP. Avg 13.0s/case, 2.64 tool calls/case, 3.64 LLM turns/case. Native llama-server tools API (--jinja). Trace shows clean reasoning + tool orchestration + JSON validation. Demonstrates Gemma 4's headlined function-calling capability while reusing 100% of the deterministic infrastructure as tool implementations.

c17 LiteRT-LM agent on existing C16 v2 fine-tune bundle: ALL chat fails (with or without tools) with "unknown method: map has no method named get (in template:236)". Bundle's embedded chat template uses Python dict.get() but LiteRT-LM 0.10.1 Jinja runtime can't render it. 0.10.1 is the latest published litert-lm version — no upgrade. Bundle is broken end-to-end at the chat-template layer, not just the extraction quality. Fix path: ship llama.cpp + base Gemma 4 E2B Q3_K_M on iOS (C12 proved this builds). Defer LiteRT-LM until Google ships a compatible runtime.

c17 RAG over CDC NNDSS / WHO IDSR (apps/mobile/convert/reportable_conditions.json, ~50 entries) + rag_search.py keyword-overlap scorer with apostrophe normalization. Wired as 4th agent tool (lookup_reportable_conditions). Full 27-case combined bench (originals 9 + adv1 5 + adv2 8 + adv3 5): agent + RAG hits 68/70 = 0.971 recall, 1.000 precision, 0.986 F1, 25/27 cases perfect, 0 FP. RAG handles the long-tail conditions (Legionnaires, C diff, RMSF, valley fever, Marburg) NOT in the curated lookup table. Each RAG hit returns source URL for provenance ("CDC NNDSS / source_url").

c17 Swift RAG mirror — apps/mobile/ios-app/ClinIQ/ClinIQ/Extraction/ReportableConditions.swift (50 entries, mirror of reportable_conditions.json) + RagSearch.swift (apostrophe normalize, phrase bonus, token-overlap F1, long-token bonus). Swift CLI bench (validate_rag.swift): 11/11 queries match Python rag_search behavior. xcodebuild PASS.

c17 iOS agent scaffold — apps/mobile/ios-app/ClinIQ/ClinIQ/Extraction/{AgentRunner.swift, GemmaToolTemplate.swift, ToolCallParser.swift}. AgentRunner orchestrates agent loop using any InferenceEngine (LlamaCppInferenceEngine works) + 3 default tools (extract_codes_from_text, lookup_reportable_conditions, validate_fhir_extraction). GemmaToolTemplate renders Gemma 4 native tool-calling chat template (system+tools+user, assistant tool_calls, tool_response) reverse-engineered from C16 bundle's embedded jinja. ToolCallParser handles 5-char `<|"|>` string sentinels + JSON-quote drift fallback. Swift CLI parser smoke test: 4/4 representative samples parsed correctly (single call, multiple calls, JSON drift, no-call final answer). xcodebuild PASS. Ready to wire ExtractionService.run() to call AgentRunner when narrative has unstructured prose.

c17 ExtractionService → AgentRunner wired. New 3-tier flow: (1) EicrPreparser deterministic short-circuit (instant, 1.000 F1 on inline/CDA/lookup cases); (2) AgentRunner.run(narrative) invoked when det.hasAnyDeterministic == false AND engine != stub — labeled "Gemma 4 agent + RAG" in UI; (3) legacy raw-LLM single-shot as final fallback. LlamaCppInferenceEngine resolver order changed: base Gemma 4 (gemma-4-E2B-it-Q3_K_M.gguf) preferred over fine-tune for agent compatibility (fine-tune emits malformed tool calls). iOS xcodebuild PASS. App installed + launched in iPhone17ProDemo simulator with 3 demo cases visible. Base GGUF seeded into Documents (2.4 GB). E2E runtime drive in simulator requires manual tap on Aisha Washington pending case (no headless touch automation in simctl).

c17 iOS UI provenance display — apps/mobile/ios-app/ClinIQ/ClinIQ/Views/Components/ProvenanceBadge.swift renders per-code tier chip (INLINE green / CDA teal / LOOKUP amber / RAG purple), confidence percentage, tap-to-expand source span + sourceURL deep-link. Wired through DraftCondition/DraftLab/DraftMedication.provenance fields populated from ParsedExtraction.matches via ReviewDraft.from(parsed:). EntityReviewRow accepts optional provenance; LabReviewRow reads lab.provenance. ExtractionService now uses EicrPreparser.extractWithProvenance + assigns matches to det.matches. ParsedExtraction grew a matches:[CodeProvenance] field. xcodebuild PASS, app installs and launches. Provenance probe CLI confirms 4-tier flow: INLINE 99% / CDA 99% / LOOKUP 85% / NegEx-suppressed TB.

c17 audio dictation — apps/mobile/ios-app/ClinIQ/ClinIQ/Audio/{SpeechDictationService.swift, DictationButton.swift}. Apple Speech framework with requiresOnDeviceRecognition=true (iOS 17+) so audio never leaves the device. SFSpeechAudioBufferRecognitionRequest streams partial transcripts to the UI; AVAudioEngine taps mic input at 1024-frame buffers and computes RMS for the live waveform meter. ContextualStrings primes the language model with 50+ medical priors (TB, mpox, RSV, valley fever, RMSF, doxycycline, ceftriaxone, etc.) for higher dictation accuracy on disease names + drugs. NSMicrophoneUsageDescription + NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription added to Info.plist. DictationButton wired into NewCaseView above the narrative TextEditor — mic toggle + live waveform + partial-transcript drawer; stitches into existing typed text without clobbering. Mirrors EpiCast's "describe in your own language" winning pattern, layered on the same agent + RAG pipeline. xcodebuild PASS, app installs + launches. Manual tap on + → dictate → "Review with AI" required to drive E2E in simulator.
